name: Kendrick Law
headline: Torn ACL erases rookie year before it starts
body:
Detroit spent a fifth-round pick on the 5-foot-11, 203-pound Kentucky transfer, the kind of late flier who needs camp reps and special-teams snaps to claw onto the radar. That path closed the moment Law tore his ACL this offseason, an injury that wipes out the entire 2026 campaign before he ever lines up. A Day 3 receiver buried on a stacked depth chart had a narrow margin for relevance even at full health; with a knee reconstruction ahead, there is nothing to project.

The ranks reflect the reality, slotting him around WR195 in redraft and roughly WR166 in dynasty, and he sits rostered in essentially no leagues. There is no usable role, no target share, and no statistical case to make. Leave him untouched in every 2026 format and revisit only if he flashes in a healthy 2027 camp.
